name: EBT — Energy-Based Transformer with Closed-Form Quadratic Refinement (Notable Non-Record)
blurb: First EBT submission to parameter-golf (0 prior PRs in keyword scan over 1,867 PRs). Adds K-step closed-form quadratic energy refinement g(h) = 0.5||A h||^2 + b.h on the final hidden state, trained with standard CE plus an auxiliary CE on h_0 (weight 0.1) to prevent refinement collapse. MLX smoke at 1k steps (2L/128d, single recycled shard) shows: train+eval refinement (K=2/K=2) vs no-refinement baseline are statistically tied at 2.236 vs 2.234 val_bpb, but disabling refinement at eval after training with it lands at 2.267 (+0.034 bpb worse than baseline) — confirming the K refinement steps do real test-time compute, not act as a regulariser. PyTorch and MLX implementations are byte-for-byte the same math via closed-form gradient (no autograd nesting). Headline 8xH100 SP1024 numbers TBD pending GPU access.
author: alexdwu13

smoke.config: NUM_LAYERS=2 MODEL_DIM=128 ENERGY_RANK=32 REFINE_STEPS_TRAIN=var REFINE_STEPS_EVAL=var ITERATIONS=1000 TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=512 TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS=4096 WARMUP_STEPS=20 WARMDOWN_ITERS=200

ablation.variant: Trained EBT, refinement OFF at eval

headline_observations: Refinement is doing real test-time work: disabling it at eval (K_eval=0 with K_train=2) is +0.034 bpb worse than the never-refined baseline.
EBT and baseline are within 0.0024 bpb at smoke scale (smaller than seed noise) — H100 capacity needed to determine Pareto outcome.
refine_relchange ~ 0.93 after 1k steps suggests refinement is overshooting; first tuning knob at scale should be REFINE_ETA_INIT and/or energy-head weight decay.

notes: Non-record submission. The 16MB artifact-size and statistical-significance criteria for SOTA records do not apply; instead, this targets the 'unique and interesting approaches' bar. val_bpb / bytes_total / val_bpb_per_seed will be populated after the 8xH100 3-seed runs (seeds 1337, 42, 2025) at the standard 9L/512d SP1024 config.
